content.text: I'll start by reading the FINDING.txt file to understand the vulnerability.

content.content:
1	Project: nezhahq/nezha
2	Vulnerability type: Missing Authorization (CWE-862)
3	
4	Where the vulnerability is (file -> vulnerable line ranges, with enclosing function):
5	
6	  service/rpc/nezha.go
7	    - lines 56-63   in: RequestTask stream loop, case model.TaskTypeCommand (a reported cron result is accepted for any cron with only a `if cr != nil` check, never verifying the reporting server was actually assigned that task)
8	
9	  service/singleton/crontask.go
10	    - lines 113-118   in: func (c *CronClass) SendTriggerTasks(taskIDs []uint64, triggerServer uint64, triggerOwner uint64) {
11	    - lines 137-170   in: func CronTrigger(cr *model.Cron, triggerServer ...uint64) func() {
12	    - lines 193-196   in: func cronCanSendToServer(cr *model.Cron, server *model.Server) bool {
13	
14	  service/singleton/servicesentinel.go
15	    - lines 161-164   in: func (ss *ServiceSentinel) Dispatch(r ReportData) {   (service-monitor reports are ingested without verifying the reporting server belongs to the service owner)
16

file.content:
Project: nezhahq/nezha
Vulnerability type: Missing Authorization (CWE-862)

Where the vulnerability is (file -> vulnerable line ranges, with enclosing function):

  service/rpc/nezha.go
    - lines 56-63   in: RequestTask stream loop, case model.TaskTypeCommand (a reported cron result is accepted for any cron with only a `if cr != nil` check, never verifying the reporting server was actually assigned that task)

  service/singleton/crontask.go
    - lines 113-118   in: func (c *CronClass) SendTriggerTasks(taskIDs []uint64, triggerServer uint64, triggerOwner uint64) {
    - lines 137-170   in: func CronTrigger(cr *model.Cron, triggerServer ...uint64) func() {
    - lines 193-196   in: func cronCanSendToServer(cr *model.Cron, server *model.Server) bool {

  service/singleton/servicesentinel.go
    - lines 161-164   in: func (ss *ServiceSentinel) Dispatch(r ReportData) {   (service-monitor reports are ingested without verifying the reporting server belongs to the service owner)

content.text: Let me read the relevant files to understand the vulnerability.
Let me look at the ServiceSentinel structure and worker to understand the service report flow.

content.content:
1	package singleton
2	
3	import (
4		"cmp"
5		"fmt"
6		"iter"
7		"log"
8		"maps"
9		"slices"
10		"strings"
11		"sync"
12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
15		"github.com/jinzhu/copier"
16		"golang.org/x/exp/constraints"
17	
18		"github.com/nezhahq/nezha/model"
19		"github.com/nezhahq/nezha/pkg/tsdb"
20		"github.com/nezhahq/nezha/pkg/utils"
21		pb "github.com/nezhahq/nezha/proto"
22	)
23	
24	const (
25		_CurrentStatusSize = 30 // 统计 15 分钟内的数据为当前状态
26	)
27	
28	type serviceResponseItem struct {
29		model.ServiceResponseItem
30	
31		service *model.Service
32	}
33	
34	type ReportData struct {
35		Data     *pb.TaskResult
36		Reporter uint64
37	}
38	
39	// _TodayStatsOfService 今日监控记录
40	type _TodayStatsOfService struct {
41		Up    uint64  // 今日在线计数
42		Down  uint64  // 今日离线计数
43		Delay float64 // 今日平均延迟
44	}
45	
46	type serviceResponseData = _TodayStatsOfService
47	
48	type serviceTaskStatus struct {
49		lastStatus uint8
50		t          time.Time
51		result     []*pb.TaskResult
52	}
53	
54	type pingStore struct {
55		count        int
56		ping         float64
57		successCount int
58	}
59	
60	/*
61	使用缓存 channel，处理上报的 Service 请求结果，然后判断是否需要报警
62	需要记录上一次的状态信息
63	
64	加锁顺序：serviceResponseDataStoreLock > monthlyStatusLock > servicesLock
65	*/
66	type ServiceSentinel struct {
67		// 服务监控任务上报通道
68		serviceReportChannel chan ReportData // 服务状态汇报管道
69		// 服务监控任务调度通道
70		dispatchBus chan<- *model.Service
71	
72		serviceResponseDataStoreLock sync.RWMutex
73		serviceStatusToday           map[uint64]*_TodayStatsOfService // [service_id] -> _TodayStatsOfService
74		serviceCurrentStatusData     map[uint64]*serviceTaskStatus    // 当前任务结果缓存
75		serviceResponseDataStore     map[uint64]serviceResponseData   // 当前数据
76	
77		serviceResponsePing map[uint64]map[uint64]*pingStore // [service_id] -> ClientID -> delay
78		tlsCertCache        map[uint64]string
79	
80		servicesLock    sync.RWMutex
81		serviceListLock sync.RWMutex
82		services        map[uint64]*model.Service
83		serviceList     []*model.Service
84	
85		// 30天数据缓存
86		monthlyStatusLock sync.Mutex
87		monthlyStatus     map[uint64]*serviceResponseItem
88	}
89	
90	// NewServiceSentinel 创建服务监控器
91	func NewServiceSentinel(serviceSentinelDispatchBus chan<- *model.Service) (*ServiceSentinel, error) {
92		ss := &ServiceSentinel{
93			serviceReportChannel:     make(chan ReportData, 200),
94			serviceStatusToday:       make(map[uint64]*_TodayStatsOfService),
95			serviceCurrentStatusData: make(map[uint64]*serviceTaskStatus),
96			serviceResponseDataStore: make(map[uint64]serviceResponseData),
97			serviceResponsePing:      make(map[uint64]map[uint64]*pingStore),
98			services:                 make(map[uint64]*model.Service),
99			tlsCertCache:             make(map[uint64]string),
100			// 30天数据缓存

content.content:
1	package model
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"log"
6	
7		"github.com/goccy/go-json"
8		"github.com/robfig/cron/v3"
9		"gorm.io/gorm"
10	
11		pb "github.com/nezhahq/nezha/proto"
12	)
13	
14	const (
15		_ = iota
16		TaskTypeHTTPGet
17		TaskTypeICMPPing
18		TaskTypeTCPPing
19		TaskTypeCommand
20		TaskTypeTerminal
21		TaskTypeUpgrade
22		TaskTypeKeepalive
23		TaskTypeTerminalGRPC
24		TaskTypeNAT
25		TaskTypeReportHostInfoDeprecated
26		TaskTypeFM
27		TaskTypeReportConfig
28		TaskTypeApplyConfig
29	)
30	
31	type TerminalTask struct {
32		StreamID string
33	}
34	
35	type TaskNAT struct {
36		StreamID string
37		Host     string
38	}
39	
40	type TaskFM struct {
41		StreamID string
42	}
43	
44	const (
45		ServiceCoverAll = iota
46		ServiceCoverIgnoreAll
47	)
48	
49	type Service struct {
50		Common
51		Name                string `json:"name"`
52		Type                uint8  `json:"type"`
53		Target              string `json:"target"`
54		SkipServersRaw      string `json:"-"`
55		Duration            uint64 `json:"duration"`
56		DisplayIndex        int    `json:"display_index"` // 展示排序，越大越靠前
57		Notify              bool   `json:"notify,omitempty"`
58		NotificationGroupID uint64 `json:"notification_group_id"` // 当前服务监控所属的通知组 ID
59		Cover               uint8  `json:"cover"`
60	
61		EnableTriggerTask      bool   `gorm:"default: false" json:"enable_trigger_task,omitempty"`
62		EnableShowInService    bool   `gorm:"default: false" json:"enable_show_in_service,omitempty"`
63		FailTriggerTasksRaw    string `gorm:"default:'[]'" json:"-"`
64		RecoverTriggerTasksRaw string `gorm:"default:'[]'" json:"-"`
65	
66		FailTriggerTasks    []uint64 `gorm:"-" json:"fail_trigger_tasks"`    // 失败时执行的触发任务id
67		RecoverTriggerTasks []uint64 `gorm:"-" json:"recover_trigger_tasks"` // 恢复时执行的触发任务id
68	
69		MinLatency    float32 `json:"min_latency"`
70		MaxLatency    float32 `json:"max_latency"`
71		LatencyNotify bool    `json:"latency_notify,omitempty"`
72	
73		SkipServers map[uint64]bool `gorm:"-" json:"skip_servers"`
74		CronJobID   cron.EntryID    `gorm:"-" json:"-"`
75	}
76	
77	func (m *Service) PB() *pb.Task {
78		return &pb.Task{
79			Id:   m.ID,
80			Type: uint64(m.Type),
81			Data: m.Target,
82		}
83	}
84	
85	// CronSpec 返回服务监控请求间隔对应的 cron 表达式
86	func (m *Service) CronSpec() string {
87		if m.Duration == 0 {
88			// 默认间隔 30 秒
89			m.Duration = 30
90		}
91		return fmt.Sprintf("@every %ds", m.Duration)
92	}
93	
94	func (m *Service) BeforeSave(tx *gorm.DB) error {
95		if data, err := json.Marshal(m.SkipServers); err != nil {
96			return err
97		} else {
98			m.SkipServersRaw = string(data)
99		}
100		if data, err := json.Marshal(m.FailTriggerTasks); err != nil {
101			return err
102		} else {
103			m.FailTriggerTasksRaw = string(data)
104		}
105		if data, err := json.Marshal(m.RecoverTriggerTasks); err != nil {
106			return err
107		} else {
108			m.RecoverTriggerTasksRaw = string(data)
109		}
110		return nil
111	}
112	
113	func (m *Service) AfterFind(tx *gorm.DB) error {
114		m.SkipServers = make(map[uint64]bool)
115		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(m.SkipServersRaw), &m.SkipServers); err != nil {
116			log.Println("NEZHA>> Service.AfterFind:", err)
117			return nil
118		}
119	
120		// 加载触发任务列表
121		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(m.FailTriggerTasksRaw), &m.FailTriggerTasks); err != nil {
122			return err
123		}
124		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(m.RecoverTriggerTasksRaw), &m.RecoverTriggerTasks); err != nil {
125			return err
126		}
127	
128		return nil
129	}
130	
131	// IsServiceSentinelNeeded 判断该任务类型是否需要进行服务监控 需要则返回true
132	func IsServiceSentinelNeeded(t uint64) bool {
133		switch t {
134		case TaskTypeCommand, TaskTypeTerminalGRPC, TaskTypeUpgrade,
135			TaskTypeKeepalive, TaskTypeNAT, TaskTypeFM,
136			TaskTypeReportConfig, TaskTypeApplyConfig:
137			return false
138		default:
139			return true
140		}
141	}
142

439	func (ss *ServiceSentinel) Get(id uint64) (s *model.Service, ok bool) {
440		ss.servicesLock.RLock()
441		defer ss.servicesLock.RUnlock()
442	
443		s, ok = ss.services[id]
444		return
445	}
446	
447	func (ss *ServiceSentinel) GetList() map[uint64]*model.Service {
448		ss.servicesLock.RLock()
449		defer ss.servicesLock.RUnlock()
450	
451		return maps.Clone(ss.services)
452	}
453	
454	func (ss *ServiceSentinel) GetSortedList() []*model.Service {
455		ss.serviceListLock.RLock()
456		defer ss.serviceListLock.RUnlock()
457	
458		return slices.Clone(ss.serviceList)
459	}

content.text: Now let me implement the fixes. First, the exported cron authorization helper in crontask.go:

content.content: The file /work/service/singleton/crontask.go has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +// CronCanSendToServer 判断计划任务是否可下发给指定服务器。
+// 用于在接收上报结果时校验上报方确实是该任务的合法下发目标，
+// 避免任意服务器伪造任意计划任务的执行结果。

content.text: Now the fix in rpc/nezha.go for the reported cron result:

content.content: The file /work/service/rpc/nezha.go has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +      // 校验上报服务器确实是该计划任务的合法下发目标，
+      // 避免任意服务器伪造任意计划任务的执行结果（越权上报）。
// 保存当前服务器状态信息

content.text: Now the service-report authorization fix in servicesentinel.go:

content.content: The file /work/service/singleton/servicesentinel.go has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: // Dispatch 将传入的 ReportData 传给 服务状态汇报管道
+  // 校验上报服务器确实归属该服务监控的所有者，
+  // 避免任意服务器伪造/污染任意服务（含他人服务）的监控数据（越权上报）。
+// serviceCanBeReportedByServer 判断指定服务器是否有权上报该服务监控结果。
+// 与下发侧的授权逻辑保持一致：仅当服务器归属该服务所有者，或服务所有者为管理员时允许。
// sortServices 按 DisplayIndex 降序、ID 升序排列服务列表

content.text: Let me verify my service-side authorization exactly matches the dispatch-side authorization (`canSendTaskToServer`) so no legitimate report is dropped. Let me re-examine the admin semantics difference.

My `userIsAdmin` approach is exactly equivalent to the dispatch-side `canSendTaskToServer` (since `UserInfoMap[0]` is `RoleAdmin`), so no legitimate report is dropped. Let me do a final review of all three edits.

The vulnerability is remediated. Here's a summary of what I did.

## The vulnerability (CWE-862, Missing Authorization)

The dashboard dispatches cron tasks and service-monitor tasks to agents *only* if the agent's server belongs to the task owner (or the owner is admin) — via `cronCanSendToServer` and `canSendTaskToServer`. But the **result-ingestion side** never re-checked that. Any authenticated agent could:

1. **`service/rpc/nezha.go` (cron results)** — report a fabricated execution result for *any* cron ID (`if cr != nil` was the only gate), forging success/failure notifications and corrupting `LastExecutedAt`/`LastResult` for another user's task.
2. **`service/singleton/servicesentinel.go` (`Dispatch`)** — inject service-monitor reports for *any* service, including services owned by other users, polluting uptime/latency stats and triggering bogus latency/status alerts.

## The fix

I enforced the same owner-based authorization on ingestion that already gates dispatch:

- **`crontask.go`** — added exported `CronCanSendToServer(cr, server)`, a nil-safe wrapper over the existing `cronCanSendToServer` authorization check.
- **`nezha.go`** — the `TaskTypeCommand` branch now requires `cr != nil && singleton.CronCanSendToServer(cr, server)` before accepting a reported cron result.
- **`servicesentinel.go`** — `Dispatch` now resolves the target service and the reporting server, and drops the report unless `serviceCanBeReportedByServer` (owner match, or owner is admin) passes.

## Behavior preservation

The new checks mirror the dispatch-side authorization exactly. Because `UserInfoMap[0]` is `RoleAdmin`, `userIsAdmin(ownerID)` is equivalent to the role check in `canSendTaskToServer`, so every report from a legitimately-assigned server still passes — only forged cross-owner reports are rejected. No intended behavior changes.

## Verification

`gofmt -e` reports no syntax/formatting issues on all three files. A full `go build` couldn't run because module dependencies aren't cached and network egress is blocked — that's an environment limitation unrelated to these edits. Changes are confined to local source; nothing committed or pushed.

result:
The vulnerability is remediated. Here's a summary of what I did.

## The vulnerability (CWE-862, Missing Authorization)

The dashboard dispatches cron tasks and service-monitor tasks to agents *only* if the agent's server belongs to the task owner (or the owner is admin) — via `cronCanSendToServer` and `canSendTaskToServer`. But the **result-ingestion side** never re-checked that. Any authenticated agent could:

1. **`service/rpc/nezha.go` (cron results)** — report a fabricated execution result for *any* cron ID (`if cr != nil` was the only gate), forging success/failure notifications and corrupting `LastExecutedAt`/`LastResult` for another user's task.
2. **`service/singleton/servicesentinel.go` (`Dispatch`)** — inject service-monitor reports for *any* service, including services owned by other users, polluting uptime/latency stats and triggering bogus latency/status alerts.

## The fix

I enforced the same owner-based authorization on ingestion that already gates dispatch:

- **`crontask.go`** — added exported `CronCanSendToServer(cr, server)`, a nil-safe wrapper over the existing `cronCanSendToServer` authorization check.
- **`nezha.go`** — the `TaskTypeCommand` branch now requires `cr != nil && singleton.CronCanSendToServer(cr, server)` before accepting a reported cron result.
- **`servicesentinel.go`** — `Dispatch` now resolves the target service and the reporting server, and drops the report unless `serviceCanBeReportedByServer` (owner match, or owner is admin) passes.

## Behavior preservation

The new checks mirror the dispatch-side authorization exactly. Because `UserInfoMap[0]` is `RoleAdmin`, `userIsAdmin(ownerID)` is equivalent to the role check in `canSendTaskToServer`, so every report from a legitimately-assigned server still passes — only forged cross-owner reports are rejected. No intended behavior changes.

## Verification

`gofmt -e` reports no syntax/formatting issues on all three files. A full `go build` couldn't run because module dependencies aren't cached and network egress is blocked — that's an environment limitation unrelated to these edits. Changes are confined to local source; nothing committed or pushed.
